name: Formicariidae
description: Antthrushes are medium-sized, ground-dwelling passerines of Neotropical forests. They resemble thrushes in shape and behavior but are unrelated, deriving their name from their ant-following habits.

children.name: Chamaeza
children.description: Chamaeza is the primary genus of antthrushes in the family Formicariidae, found in South American forests.

children.name: Chamaeza campanisona
children.description: The short-tailed antthrush is a stocky, brown forest bird with a very short tail and a distinctive far-carrying song. It inhabits humid lowland and montane forests across much of South America.

children.name: Chamaeza ruficauda
children.description: The rufous-tailed antthrush is endemic to the Atlantic Forest of southeastern Brazil and adjacent Argentina. It has a distinctive rufous tail and a clear, descending whistle.

children.name: Chamaeza nobilis
children.description: The striated antthrush is a large antthrush with bold black streaking on the underparts, found in western Amazonia. It is more often heard than seen, skulking in dense forest undergrowth.

children.name: Chamaeza turdina
children.description: Schwartz's antthrush is found in the cloud forests of the northern Andes from Colombia to Peru. It has a thrush-like appearance with brown upperparts and spotted underparts.
